audit_items.proof_trace: Both `ldap_create_assertion_control_value` (L34) and `ldap_create_assertion_control` (L84) dereference the `ld` parameter (`ld->ld_errno`) without first checking `ld != NULL`. Peer functions in the same library — `psearchctrl.c:87` (`ldap_create_persistentsearch_control_value`) and `sortctrl.c:299` (`ldap_create_sort_control_value`) — both guard with `if (ld == NULL) return LDAP_PARAM_ERROR;` before any dereference. The inconsistency means a NULL `ld` causes an immediate crash in assertion.c where comparable code would return an error code.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An LDAP client application that fails to verify `ldap_initialize()` returned a non-NULL handle (e.g., due to a connection error or resource exhaustion) and then calls `ldap_create_assertion_control` or `ldap_create_assertion_control_value` will crash at the first `ld->ld_errno` write; an attacker who can induce initialization failure (e.g., by exhausting connections) could trigger application DoS through this path.
